sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 1.4 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.1)

statement.latex: (A.\,I.\,Mal'cev). Does there exist a ring without zero divisors which is not em\-bed\-dable in a skew field, while the multiplicative semigroup of its non-zero elements is embeddable in a group? {{ L.\,A.\,Bokut'}} Yes, such groups do exist (L.\,A.\,Bokut', Soviet Math. Dokl., { 8} (1967), 901--904; \ A.\,Bowtell, J.~Algebra, { 7} (1967), 126--139; \ A.\,Klein, J.~Algebra, { 7} (1967), 100--125)....

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
